description_long: This object is a rustic, cylindrical flute crafted from a pale, bone-like material. The body is straight and slightly tapered, with a natural, matte finish and subtle surface texture. Along one side, there are several evenly spaced finger holes, and both ends are open, with one end showing a slightly flared and rougher edge. The flute lacks any visible keywork or metallic fittings, giving it a primitive and handmade appearance. The coloration is a muted beige with some darker patches and natural imperfections, suggesting it may be made from animal bone or a similar organic material. There are no visible engravings, embellishments, or branding on its surface.
description: A straight, bone-colored flute with finger holes and open ends, featuring a natural, unadorned finish and a slightly rough texture.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the flute, with visible finger holes and a slightly chipped end.
description_view_1: End-on view displaying the circular, hollow opening and the gentle taper of the flute's body.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the smooth, unadorned surface and the alignment of the finger holes.
description_view_3: End-on view from the opposite end, showing the flared, rougher edge and the hollow interior.
